title: AI Videos in Hungary's Election
summary: In Hungary, a fake video made with AI was shared by Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's party before an election. The video shows a soldier being executed and tries to scare people about the war in Ukraine. It targets Péter Magyar, who is running against Orbán. The video says Magyar wants Hungary to join the war, but Magyar says that is not true. Fact-checkers say using AI to make fake videos is new in elections. This story is about how technology can be used to trick people during important votes.

keywords.explanation: artificial intelligence, a computer technology that can make videos or pictures that look real but are fake
a vote where people choose who will lead their country
not real, made to trick people
a moving picture that tells a story
a big fight between countries, like the one in Ukraine

questions.question: Who shared the fake AI video in Hungary?
What does the fake video show?
Who is the video trying to scare people about?
What does Magyar say about the war in Ukraine?
options: He does not want Hungary to join it
He wants Hungary to join it
He does not know about it
questions.correct_answer: He does not want Hungary to join it

questions.question: What is new about this video according to fact-checkers?
options: It uses AI to make fake content
It is a real news story
questions.correct_answer: It uses AI to make fake content

questions.question: Why is this story important?
options: It shows how technology can trick people in elections
It is about a fun movie
questions.correct_answer: It shows how technology can trick people in elections

background_read: This article is about Hungary, a country in Europe, where an election is happening. Elections are when adults vote to choose their leaders. Sometimes, people use computers to make fake videos that look real, called AI videos, to influence votes. This can be misleading because it spreads false information. It's important to check facts and not believe everything you see online, especially during elections.
Article_Structure: WHO: Viktor Orbán's party and Péter Magyar
WHAT: A fake AI video about a soldier's execution used in an election
WHY: To scare voters and influence the election against Magyar

perspectives.description: Orbán's party uses the video to warn that Magyar might involve Hungary in war.
Magyar says the video is fake and he does not want war.
Journalists say using AI for fake videos is a new and worrying trend in elections.
